description:
A technical-writer on your team needs a concise, automatically-generated
    overview of a few critical command-line tools that are **already installed**
    on the workstation.  
    Your job is to create that overview, in *exactly* the layout described
    below, so it can be copy-pasted directly into wider end-user
    documentation.

    1. Create the directory  
       /home/user/docs  
       if it does not already exist.

    2. Inside that directory, write a file named  
       /home/user/docs/pkg_overview.md  

    3. The file **must contain information about precisely these three
       Debian/Ubuntu packages, in the order shown**:  
       • bash  
       • coreutils  
       • grep  

    4. Pull the **currently-installed version** and the **one-line
       synopsis/short description** for each package directly from the
       package database (for example, via `dpkg-query`).
       Do **not** hard-code the values; the document has to reflect whatever
       is installed on the system at execution time.

    5. The Markdown file **must follow this rigid format** so that an
       automated test can parse it unambiguously:

       Line 1  : `# Package Overview`  
       Line 2  : *(blank line)*  
       For **each** package repeat the following 4-line block, with a blank
       line separating successive blocks:

           Line A : `## <package-name>`
           Line B : `Version: <installed-version-string>`
           Line C : `Description: <single-line short description>`
           Line D : *(blank line)*

       Concretely, the finished file will look like the example below,
       but with the actual version numbers and descriptions retrieved
       from the system’s package database:

       # Package Overview

       ## bash  
       Version: 5.1.16-2ubuntu1  
       Description: GNU Bourne Again SHell  

       ## coreutils  
       Version: 8.32-4.1ubuntu1  
       Description: GNU core utilities  

       ## grep  
       Version: 3.7-1build1  
       Description: GNU grep, egrep and fgrep  

       ── Note ──  
       • The two leading “#” marks and all colons must be present exactly as
         shown.  
       • Each “Version:” and “Description:” line must be on **one single line**.  
       • There must be **one blank line** after every 4-line block (including
         after the last block).  

    6. When you are finished, the directory tree should contain exactly one
       new file, `/home/user/docs/pkg_overview.md`, whose content adheres to
       the specification above.
truth:
expected_directory: /home/user/docs
    expected_file     : /home/user/docs/pkg_overview.md

    # Content rules the test harness will enforce
    rule_1: First line must be exactly "# Package Overview"
    rule_2: Second line must be completely empty
    rule_3: There must be exactly three package sections, in this order:
            "bash", "coreutils", "grep"
    rule_4: Each section must have four lines structured as:
            • "## <pkg>"
            • "Version: <non-empty>"
            • "Description: <non-empty>"
            • empty line
    rule_5: The test harness will run
            dpkg-query -W -f='${Version}\t${binary:Summary}\n' \
               bash coreutils grep
            and compare each resulting <Version> and <Description> field
            against the corresponding lines in pkg_overview.md.
